subject: update DMaaP ZooKeeper version
commit_message:
update DMaaP ZooKeeper version

Issue-ID: DMAAP-1007
Change-Id: I3e69858143d3ae738989c77b3e156256a9aa706f
Signed-off-by: sunil unnava <sunil.unnava@att.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/oom-master-verify-helm/1539/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/oom-master-verify-helm/1539

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

kubectl get pods --all-namespaces
and
oom/kubernetes/robot$ sudo ./ete-k8s.sh onap health

Patch Set 1:

Are you talking about the data router health check. Can you point the application.

Patch Set 1:

Normally when you put up a patch - you check that it runs in your kubernetes cluster - just paste your results here.

when you bring up your dmaap pods to test them in your kubernetes cluster make sure robot is also deployed - try the following

after that navigate to oom/kubernetes/robot
run
sudo ./ete-k8s.sh $ENVIRON health

where $ENVIRON = onap
usually - depending on your environment

and run the healthcheck - you will get 29+ failures but make sure the dmaap ones pass

there is an example below
http://jenkins.onap.info/job/oom-cd-master2-aws/101/console
 
that partially passes
22:32:39 Basic DMAAP Data Router Health Check                                  [ WARN ] Retrying (Retry(total=2, connect=None, read=None, redirect=None, status=None)) after connection broken by 'NewConnectionError('<urllib3.connection.HTTPConnection object at 0x7f1be5723310>: Failed to establish a new connection: [Errno 111] Connection refused',)': /internal/fetchProv
22:32:39 [ WARN ] Retrying (Retry(total=1, connect=None, read=None, redirect=None, status=None)) after connection broken by 'NewConnectionError('<urllib3.connection.HTTPConnection object at 0x7f1be5723a50>: Failed to establish a new connection: [Errno 111] Connection refused',)': /internal/fetchProv
22:32:39 [ WARN ] Retrying (Retry(total=0, connect=None, read=None, redirect=None, status=None)) after connection broken by 'NewConnectionError('<urllib3.connection.HTTPConnection object at 0x7f1be5723c10>: Failed to establish a new connection: [Errno 111] Connection refused',)': /internal/fetchProv
22:32:39 | FAIL |
22:32:39 ConnectionError: HTTPConnectionPool(host='dmaap-dr-node.onap', port=8080): Max retries exceeded with url: /internal/fetchProv (Caused by NewConnectionError('<urllib3.connection.HTTPConnection object at 0x7f1be5723d90>: Failed to establish a new connection: [Errno 111] Connection refused',))
22:32:39 ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
22:32:39 Basic DMAAP Message Router Health Check                               | PASS |


you would need a PASS on both

There is also example code in 
https://git.onap.org/logging-analytics/tree/deploy/cd.sh#n426

Patch Set 1:

Michael, DataRouter and Message Router are independent applications under DMaaP. 

This change is applicable only to the Message Router. There are serveral tickets(DMAAP-964 is created by you) created to address the DataRouter healthcheck issue.

Patch Set 1:

Michael, I can't run the et8.k8h script. I am getting the below error. Can you share me the /var/opt/OpenECOMP_ETE/runTags.sh. As the developer is asked to run the script. It really helps if there is a documentation on how to run the et8.k8h

Patch Set 1:

ubuntu@mr-k8s:~/tmp/oom/kubernetes/robot$ sudo ./ete-k8s.sh onap health
./ete-k8s.sh: 22: [: onap: unexpected operator
./ete-k8s.sh: 22: [: health: unexpected operator
+ export NAMESPACE=onap
+ POD=viable-heron-message-router-0
+ TAGS=-i health
+ ETEHOME=/var/opt/OpenECOMP_ETE
+ kubectl --namespace onap exec viable-heron-message-router-0 -- bash -c ls -1q /share/logs/ | wc -l
ls: cannot access '/share/logs/': No such file or directory
+ export GLOBAL_BUILD_NUMBER=0
+ printf %04d 0
+ OUTPUT_FOLDER=0000_ete_health
+ DISPLAY_NUM=90
+ VARIABLEFILES=-V /share/config/vm_properties.py -V /share/config/integration_robot_properties.py -V /share/config/integration_preload_parameters.py
+ VARIABLES=-v GLOBAL_BUILD_NUMBER:10994
+ kubectl --namespace onap exec viable-heron-message-router-0 -- /var/opt/OpenECOMP_ETE/runTags.sh -V /share/config/vm_properties.py -V /share/config/integration_robot_properties.py -V /share/config/integration_preload_parameters.py -v GLOBAL_BUILD_NUMBER:10994 -d /share/logs/0000_ete_health -i health --display 90
rpc error: code = 2 desc = oci runtime error: exec failed: container_linux.go:247: starting container process caused "exec: \"/var/opt/OpenECOMP_ETE/runTags.sh\": stat /var/opt/OpenECOMP_ETE/runTags.sh: no such file or directory"

command terminated with exit code 126

Patch Set 1:

running robot tests requires the robot container to be up
did you add --set robot.enabled=true 
or enable it in your yaml override
the script is oom/kubernetes/robot/ete-k8s.sh

instructions in  (don;'t know where in RTD)
https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/ONAP+Development#ONAPDevelopment-RunningRobotCommands

and script example in
https://git.onap.org/logging-analytics/tree/deploy/cd.sh#n426

Patch Set 1:

for help check  https://gerrit.onap.org/r/#/c/75479/

Patch Set 1:

DMaaP message router and data router are separate projects under DMaaP and this change is one of the message router project. So this change will not impact the data router
